focus: Resolve seam continuity with minimal edits.
prev_tail_excerpt:
...as an opinion, not kindness, but it belonged to Drouet and not to the prison, which made it feel larger than it should have.

Outside, the light struck hard off the paving stones. The women with baskets had changed places with another line of women, newer parcels, same faces of waiting worn into different shapes. Elise moved into the narrow strip of shade along the wall and opened the notebook just enough to see the first page.

Lucien's hand was as she remembered it: legible even in haste, each entry given room enough to remain human.

Nadir Belkacem. 3 June. Arrested on delivery, not in flight. Unarmed. The escape was invented afterward. Body not returned.

Abel Saint-Clair, nineteen. 4 June. Printer. Burn marks on two fingertips. Dead in the tollhouse cellar before the papers made him a doctrine.

Henri Vasseur. 27 March. Taken alive. Sober. Reported missing after, though he had not gone anywhere by choice.

Below the names were dates, prisons, wrong spellings crossed out and corrected, burial grounds, the names of mothers and sisters where Lucien had been able to find them. He had refused the state's cheaper labels: unknown female, Arab male, agitator dead in police action. Each line gave a name, a date, where the body had been taken, and how the lie was entered after.

The book in her bodice weighed almost nothing. The pressure of it on the wound made every step toward the street distinct. The quarry map had once been the object that mattered, a way through stone, a route for bodies still alive enough to move. This was smaller, meaner, and in its way more final. It offered no path back, no clever exit, no hidden turn under the city. Only the work of not letting the state finish the theft it had begun by deciding, in prison and on paper, what else could be withheld.
